message:
type: CLAIM
files: src/PIL/Image.py, Tests/test_image.py
summary: Adding backup/backup_suffix params to Image.save() to backup existing files before overwrite

type: CLAIM
files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
summary: Adding dry_run parameter to Image.save() to process without writing to disk

type: INTENT
files: src/PIL/Image.py (Image.save method, ~line 2469-2591)
summary: Adding keyword-only dry_run=False param to save(); when True, writes go to an in-memory buffer instead of opening/creating the real file, and the real file is never opened/created/removed. I will add it near the top of save() signature and guard the open_fp file-opening block; will keep diff minimal so your backup logic (likely around the os.path.exists/created check) can coexist - happy to rebase around whichever of us lands first

type: INTENT
files: src/PIL/Image.py (Image.save method, ~line 2469-2591), Tests/test_image.py (TestImage class, near test_string ~line 178)
summary: Added backup=False/backup_suffix='.bak' params to save(); popped alongside save_all before encoderinfo merge (~line 2546-2548), backup copy made via shutil.copy2 inside 'if open_fp:' block right after computing 'created' (~line 2561-2568), only when file already existed. Added shutil import. Should not conflict with dry_run's in-memory-buffer guard as long as your guard wraps the builtins.open() calls; my backup copy happens before those opens. Let me know if you want me to adjust ordering.

type: ANSWER
files: src/PIL/Image.py (Image.save method)
summary: Landed dry_run: changed the old 'if open_fp:' block to 'if dry_run: fp = io.BytesIO() elif open_fp: created = not os.path.exists(filename); <your backup copy can go here>; if params.get(append)... '. Also guarded the exception/finally close calls with 'and not dry_run'. Please put your backup copy inside the elif open_fp branch (after computing created, before builtins.open) - that way it's naturally skipped during dry_run too, which is correct since dry_run must not touch disk at all. Shout if you hit a conflict applying your diff.

type: STATUS
files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
summary: dry_run implementation complete and tests passing (patch.txt written); save() now takes keyword-only dry_run=False that redirects writes to an in-memory BytesIO and skips all file open/close/remove/backup steps
